# HappyTooth Dental Clinics

> Specialist dental clinics in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India. Two locations: HappyTooth Smile Studio (Murukkumpuzha) and HappyTooth Dental Care & Root Canal Centre (Kallambalam). Led by MDS Endodontist Dr. Thushara Sudhakaran. Tagline: "Saving Teeth. Designing Smiles."

## Overview

HappyTooth is a multi-specialist dental group in Thiruvananthapuram (Trivandrum), Kerala, operating two clinics. Every patient is seen by a specialist in the relevant field — not a general dentist filling in. The group has 18+ specialist doctors across both locations covering 8 dental specialisations.

**What makes HappyTooth different:**
- Specialist-led care across all 8 dental disciplines — patients are never treated by a generalist working outside their field
- In-house MDS Endodontist on site (not a referral) for root canal treatment
- "Save the tooth first" philosophy — extraction is the last resort, not the default
- Modern digital diagnostics: intraoral scanner, digital X-rays, OPG, CBCT referral
- Hospital-grade sterilisation between every patient
- Pain-free approach using modern anaesthesia — treats patients with dental anxiety
- Languages spoken: Malayalam, English, Hindi

---

## Clinics

### HappyTooth Smile Studio — Murukkumpuzha (Flagship)

**Address:** Ground Floor, Mangalasseri Tower, Murukkumpuzha, Thiruvananthapuram – 695302, Kerala, India
**Phone:** +91 73561 00602
**WhatsApp:** wa.me/917356100602
**Email:** smile@happytoothdental.in
**Hours:** Monday–Sunday, 9:30 AM – 6:30 PM (Open all 7 days including weekends and public holidays)
**Speciality:** Full-spectrum specialist studio — cosmetic dentistry, implants, orthodontics, endodontics, smile design, whitening, paediatric dentistry
**Parking:** Ample private parking (fits 6+ cars)
**Nearby areas served:** Murukkumpuzha, Kazhakoottam, Attingal, Pothencode, Varkala, Kovalam, Trivandrum city

### HappyTooth Dental Care & Root Canal Centre — Kallambalam (Established 2021)

**Address:** Alinmoodu, Kallambalam–Varkala Road, Kallambalam, Thiruvananthapuram – 695605, Kerala, India
**Phone:** +91 87144 70808
**WhatsApp:** wa.me/918714470808
**Email:** smile@happytoothdental.in
**Hours:** Monday–Saturday, 9:30 AM – 6:30 PM (Closed Sundays)
**Speciality:** Root canal treatment, conservative dentistry, family dental care, general dentistry
**Nearby areas served:** Kallambalam, Varkala, Kazhakkoottam, Nedumangad, southern Thiruvananthapuram district

---

## Clinical Director

**Dr. Thushara Sudhakaran, BDS, MDS**
Specialist Endodontist & Clinical Director, HappyTooth Dental Clinics

- **Qualification:** BDS + MDS in Endodontics & Conservative Dentistry
- **Postgraduate institution:** A B Shetty Memorial Institute of Dental Sciences, Mangalore (2014–2017)
- **Prior practice:** Apollo White Dental, Hyderabad (2017–2021) — specialist endodontic practice serving NRI and national patients
- **Registration:** Kerala Dental Council (registered dental practitioner)
- **Expertise:** Root canal treatment (RCT), root canal retreatment (Re-RCT), complex and calcified canals, single-sitting RCT, endodontic microsurgery
- **Volume:** 1,000+ root canal treatments completed
- **Founded:** Kallambalam clinic in 2021; Murukkumpuzha Smile Studio in 2026

---

## Services

HappyTooth offers 14 specialist dental treatments across both clinics.

### 1. Root Canal Treatment (Endodontics)
Performed by an in-house MDS Endodontist. Uses rotary nickel-titanium files and, where required, magnification for precision. Single-sitting RCT available for straightforward cases; complex cases (molars, curved canals, retreatments) may need two visits. Post-RCT crown fitting included in treatment plan.

**Suitable for:** Severe or worsening toothache, sensitivity to heat/cold, pain on biting, abscess, darkened tooth after trauma.
**What to expect:** Local anaesthesia, no pain during procedure, mild soreness for 1–2 days. Most patients return to normal the next day.

### 2. Smile Designing
Customised combination of veneers, crowns, whitening, gum correction, and orthodontic adjustments planned as a cohesive treatment. Digital smile preview before any clinical work begins. Treats: stained teeth, chips, uneven edges, gaps, asymmetry, gummy smile, worn teeth, mismatched restorations.

**Timeline:** Simple (whitening + veneers): 2–3 visits over 2–3 weeks. Comprehensive: 4–8 weeks.

### 3. Aligners & Braces (Orthodontics)
Three options: traditional metal braces, ceramic (tooth-coloured) braces, and clear removable aligners. Children assessed from age 7–8. Adults of any age eligible if gums and bone are healthy.

**Duration:** Mild corrections: 6–9 months. Moderate: 12–18 months. Complex: 18–24+ months.

### 4. Teeth Whitening
Professional in-clinic whitening. Single appointment. Calibrated to starting shade — natural-bright result. Take-home maintenance kit available. Best for: extrinsic staining from food, drink, tobacco, age-related yellowing.

**Longevity:** 12–24 months with normal habits.

### 5. Paediatric Dentistry (Children's Dental Care)
Children seen from the first tooth eruption. Unhurried, fear-free appointments. Services: first check-ups, cavity treatment, pit-and-fissure sealants, fluoride application, space maintainers, habit correction appliances (thumb-sucking, mouth breathing), early orthodontic assessment.

**First visit age:** Ideally by age 1, or within 6 months of first tooth eruption.

### 6. Dental Implants
Titanium root-form implant + ceramic crown. Digital X-ray assessment before all cases; CBCT referral for complex anatomy. Assessment covers bone volume, sinus proximity, nerve canal position.

**Candidacy:** Most healthy adults. Requires adequate bone volume; active gum disease, uncontrolled diabetes, and smoking must be addressed first.
**Timeline:** 3–6 months healing after placement, then crown fitting. Total: 4–6 months from placement.
**Longevity:** 15–25+ years with proper maintenance.

### 7. Crowns & Bridges
Zirconia and full-ceramic crowns for strength and aesthetics. Crown: two appointments (prep + fitting). Bridge: fixed non-removable replacement for missing teeth using adjacent teeth as anchors.

**Materials:** Zirconia (back teeth, high force); full-ceramic (front teeth, most aesthetic).

### 8. Dental Veneers
Ultra-thin ceramic or composite shells bonded to front tooth surfaces. Treats: chips, worn edges, staining, gaps, minor misalignment, uneven shape.

**Porcelain veneers:** Laboratory-fabricated, highly stain-resistant. Longevity: 10–20 years.
**Composite veneers:** Single appointment, more economical. Longevity: 5–8 years.

### 9. Gum Care & Periodontics
From scaling and root planing (deep cleaning under local anaesthesia) to periodontal flap surgery for advanced cases. Structured maintenance programme (every 3–4 months) after active treatment.

**Signs of gum disease:** Bleeding on brushing, red/puffy gums, persistent bad breath, gum recession, loose teeth.

### 10. Scaling & Polishing (Preventive)
Removes calculus (hardened plaque) that brushing cannot. Polishing removes surface staining. Frequency: every 6 months for most adults; every 3–4 months for patients with gum disease history or diabetes. Duration: 45–60 minutes.

### 11. Extractions & Wisdom Teeth (Oral Surgery)
Simple and surgical extractions. Wisdom teeth: assessment via periapical X-ray or OPG before extraction. Recovery from surgical wisdom tooth removal: 4–7 days swelling, full recovery within 2 weeks.

### 12. Dentures & Full Mouth Rehabilitation
Complete and partial dentures; implant-supported fixed or removable prosthetics. Full mouth rehabilitation combines implants, crowns, bridges, and orthodontics to restore severely compromised dentitions.

**Timeline:** Conventional dentures: 4–6 appointments over 4–6 weeks. Full mouth rehab: 3–9 months depending on complexity.

### 13. Digital X-Rays & Diagnostics
Digital intraoral X-rays (80–90% less radiation than conventional film), OPG panoramic X-ray, intraoral scanner for 3D digital impressions, CBCT referral for implant planning and complex cases.

### 14. Emergency Dental Care
Same-day appointments for acute cases. Handles: severe toothache and abscesses, fractured teeth, avulsed (knocked-out) teeth, lost/broken crowns and bridges, soft tissue injuries.

**Avulsed tooth:** Keep moist in milk or saline (not tap water) and come in immediately.
**Emergency numbers:** Murukkumpuzha: +91 73561 00602 | Kallambalam: +91 87144 70808

---

## Pricing & Payment

- **Pricing:** Transparent cost estimate provided before treatment begins. Multi-step treatments broken down stage by stage.
- **Payment methods:** UPI (GPay, PhonePe, all UPI apps), debit cards, credit cards, cash, international Visa/Mastercard
- **Insurance:** Most Indian health insurance policies cover dental only for trauma or surgical cases — not routine care. Itemised bills provided for all treatments for insurer submission.

---

## International & NRI Patients

HappyTooth regularly treats NRI patients, tourists, and patients from the Maldives (Thiruvananthapuram is one of the closest Indian cities to the Maldives by air).

**Process for international patients:**
1. Contact on WhatsApp before travel — share X-rays and reports digitally
2. Receive treatment plan and cost estimate before arriving
3. Appointment confirmed in advance; treatment delivered as planned
4. Documentation (records, X-rays, prescriptions) provided for continuity with home dentist
5. WhatsApp follow-up available after return home

**Practical:** Open 7 days. International cards accepted. English, Malayalam, Hindi spoken. Accessible from Trivandrum International Airport.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Does a root canal hurt?**
No. Root canals are performed under local anaesthesia — the tooth is fully numb during treatment. Most patients describe it as no more uncomfortable than a filling. The procedure relieves the severe pain caused by an infected tooth.

**How many sittings does a root canal take?**
Many cases complete in one sitting. Complex teeth (molars, curved roots, calcified canals, retreatments) may need two visits. A crown is needed after the root canal to protect the tooth — this is a separate appointment.

**Do I need an appointment or can I walk in?**
Walk-ins are welcome, but booking ahead is strongly recommended — especially for root canals, crowns, and other time-intensive treatments. Emergency cases: call first, we make every effort to see the same day.

**What should I bring to my first visit?**
Previous dental records, X-rays, and a list of current medications if you have them. No complicated paperwork required.

**Do you treat children?**
Yes. Paediatric dentistry from age 1 upward. Fear-free approach; parents welcome to stay in the room.

**Do you offer clear aligners?**
Yes — for mild to moderate misalignment in adults. More complex corrections or significant bite issues are better handled with braces. Assessment determines which applies.

**How often should I get my teeth cleaned?**
Every 6 months for most adults. Every 3–4 months if you have a history of gum disease, diabetes, or heavy calculus buildup.

**Is there parking?**
Yes — ample private parking at Murukkumpuzha (6+ cars). Street parking near Kallambalam clinic.

**What languages do you speak?**
Malayalam, English, and Hindi.

**Are you open on Sundays?**
Murukkumpuzha Smile Studio: yes, open all 7 days. Kallambalam clinic: closed Sundays.
